module_mood_contract: unleashed leverage — 'we can automate the loop.' Human role shifts one level up: design the loop, name its blind spots, own what it cannot see.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block line 23: 'Placement: Agents 101 Module 6, after exercises/eval-loop.md and before the closer lectures/new-human-role-in-the-loop.md. A recognition beat: it names a pattern the student just lived (the flatlining count in the loop), so it lands after the exercise, never before.' Confirmed in evaluations.md sequence (line 35-36): eval-loop.md → this lecture → new-human-role-in-the-loop.md. Pattern-naming = closer; placement is correct.
This lecture is a closer (after the exercise), not an opener before a contrast-mood exercise. Rule 2 governs pre-exercise dosage; not applicable to a post-exercise recognition beat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named; mood floor 8+/10 on teaching beats.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Trace phase_index 1 mood_score: 9/10. Close mood_score: 9/10. Mood note: 'Your judgment comes back in' aligns directly with M6 unleashed-leverage contract. Lecture reframes the flat score as the judge's frame limit (not the student's ceiling) — empowering, not cautionary. Maintainer block names mood target: 'empowered recognition, not caution.' Teaching beat (names a discipline) → mood floor applies and is met.

Lines 5-7: flat score explained as judge's scope limitation ('blind to everything else'), not as student error. Lines 13-15: 'the moment the easy signal runs out and your judgment comes back in' frames residual work as student agency, not student failure. No blame register anywhere in the prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opening leads with observable pattern ('the count probably went quiet... Round four looked like round three') not with a failure-word. Discipline named: reading past the metric, restoring judgment where the signal ends. The failure mode (misreading a flat score as 'done') is introduced as the negative case the discipline corrects (line 5: 'It is tempting to read that as the work is done. That is not what happened.'), not as the lead frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity: front-run check, Big Idea fidelity, module-to-module arc, mood doesn't resolve early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Front-run check: the lecture does not name the human role (that is the closer's job). Line 21 explicitly defers: 'That is the question the next lecture opens.' Big Idea fidelity: M6's Big Idea is the fixed-judge eval loop as infrastructure; this lecture names one thing the fixed judge cannot see (qualitative improvement), which is precisely the setup for the human-role closer — faithful to the arc. Module-to-module arc: line 21 is a clean handoff to new-human-role-in-the-loop.md. Mood doesn't resolve early: lecture ends on a deferred question, not a landing; forward pull intact. All four facets: PASS.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture surface — no Key Concepts section. Strategy judge owns §5 on module files; N/A here.
No Key Concepts section on a lecture. Strategy judge owns §6 on module files.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No What You'll Learn section on a lecture. Strategy judge owns §7 on module files.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompts and no forcing-function mechanics — it is a pure recognition beat. Rule 16 applies to exercise phases with prompts. N/A here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches work; trainer manages room. No authored conversations or pause cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Full prose scan: no 'discuss with,' no 'share with the room,' no 'take 90 seconds,' no comparison prompts, no pause cues. The body consists entirely of recognition prose about the pattern the student just lived. Trainer cohort management is fully external to the text.
